title: Undercut/overcut pressure features from estimated post-stop rejoin battles
summary: Game-theoretic F1 strategy work shows competition materially changes optimal pit timing; ignoring opponents can reduce winning odds.
rationale: Game-theoretic F1 strategy work shows competition materially changes optimal pit timing; ignoring opponents can reduce winning odds. The current baseline has little explicit undercut/traffic modeling, so causal rival-pressure signals are a distinct next step.
implementation_hint: At each lap, estimate post-stop rejoin position using only current-lap field order and historical/OOF pit-loss priors by race-year. Build features like nearest rival gaps in front/behind, count of cars within the expected rejoin band, whether stopping now avoids emerging into traffic, and whether one more lap increases rejoin congestion or undercut threat.
expected_effect: Should help separate 'can continue' from 'must pit now before traffic/undercut closes the window,' especially in midfield strategy battles.
risk: Using realized future positions or future pit behavior will leak badly; any rejoin estimate must be based only on current standings plus training-fold priors.
